text: Strength training programs produce a large beneficial effect on running economy in highly trained middle- and long-distance runners.
population: highly trained middle- and long-distance runners (VO2max >60 ml/kg/min)

text: A strength training program performed 2-3 times per week for 8-12 weeks is sufficient to improve running economy in highly trained runners.
population: highly trained middle- and long-distance runners (VO2max >60 ml/kg/min)

text: Low to moderate resistance training intensities (40-70% of one repetition maximum) are effective for improving running economy in highly trained runners.
population: highly trained middle- and long-distance runners (VO2max >60 ml/kg/min)

text: An effective strength training volume for improving running economy consists of 2-4 resistance lower-body exercises plus up to 200 jumps and 5-10 short sprints per session.
specific_value: 2-4 lower-body exercises + up to 200 jumps + 5-10 sprints
population: highly trained middle- and long-distance runners (VO2max >60 ml/kg/min)

text: Combining resistance exercises with plyometric exercises is an appropriate strategy to improve running economy in distance runners.
population: highly trained middle- and long-distance runners (VO2max >60 ml/kg/min)
